original: Augustinian monastic order constituted at the Lecceto Monastery when Pope Alexander IV issues papal bull Licet ecclesiae catholicae
Flemish forces conquer Middelburg in Zeeland
Religious reformers John Wycliffe and Jan Hus are condemned as heretics at the Council of Constance

Spanish Pope Alexander VI decrees in the papal bull "Inter caetera" that all lands west of a line 100 leagues west of any of the islands of the Azores or the Cape Verde Islands belong to Castile, ultimately dividing the Americas between Spain and Portugal
Venice and Turkey sign Treaty of Constantinople
Dutch towns of Flushing and Veere side with Dutch rebels, the Sea Beggers (Geuzen) during the uprising against the Spanish
Peter Minuit becomes director-general of New Netherlands
Johannes van Walbeeck's fleet of four ships leaves Holland tasked with taking Curaçao from the Spanish
Battle of Etampes: French army under Turenne beats Fronde rebels
Municipality of Ilagan is founded in the Philippines
William IV Prince of Orange appointed Stadtholder of Overijssel
Rhode Island declares independence from Great Britain
Charles Bunbury on Diomed wins 1st Epsom Derby
Henry C. Overing buys 80 acres of Throggs Neck in the Bronx
Bourbon reign restored in France
Netherlands & Britain sign treaty against illegal slave handling
The Cunard Steamship Company Ltd forms San Bonifacio
Great Britain annexes Natal
US state of Michigan ends the death penalty
NY State creates a Board of Commissioners of Emigration
War of Reform (Mexico); Liberals establish capital at Vera Cruz
Cornwall Railway opens across the Royal Albert Bridge, linking the English counties of Devon and Cornwall
At Gretna, Louisiana, one of 1st guns of Rebel navy is cast
U.S. Civil War: Battle of Chancellorsville, action at Salem Church; Union Army withdraws
-16] actions at Drewry's Bluff, Virginia
Battle of Citronelle, Alabama; Confederate General Richard Taylor surrenders to Union General Edward Canby
Woodward's Gardens opens to the public in San Francisco
World's largest book, the "Kuthodaw Inscription Shrines," recording the whole of Buddhist scriptures on 729 marble tablets, is completed and opened to the public in Mandalay, Burma
Cincinnati Red Stockings play their first official baseball game, defeating the Great Western Base Ball Club 45-9
Japanese Imperial forces win the naval Battle of Hakodate over an Ezo Republic fleet
1st baseball league game (National Association of Baseball Players), (Ft Wayne 2, Cleveland 0) Deacon Jim White gets 1st hit, a double
John Gordon Cashman publishes the first edition of the "Vicksburg Evening Post" in Mississippi
Haymarket riot in Chicago; bomb kills 7 policemen
Italy and Spain sign a military agreement
Cowboy Bill Pickett invents bulldogging, the skill of grabbing cattle by the horns and wrestling them to the ground
Wilhelm Kienzl's opera "Der Evangelimann" (The Evangelist), 1st performed at the Neues Königliches Opernhaus in Berlin, Germany
First edition of the London Daily Mail, costs a halfpenny
Grease fire ignites half ton of dynamite at Cripple Creek, Colorado
Fire in Paris bazaar at Rue Jean Goujon kills 200
United States begins construction of the Panama Canal
Canadian Currency Act, 1910, receives Royal Assent
Italian mariners occupy Turkish Island of Rhodes
Italy leaves the Triple Alliance with Austria-Hungary and Germany
At request of US, Germany curtails its submarine warfare
Ned Daly, Willie Pearse, Michael O'Hanrahan and Joseph Plunkett are executed by British authorities following the Easter Rising, at Kilmainham Gaol, Dublin
A flotilla of US destroyer ships arrive in Queenstown, Ireland, to aid in convoying ships to England
Demonstrates organised by students erupt in China, after news from the Paris Peace Conference that the Allies intend to give Shangtung to Japan
First legal Sunday baseball game in NYC, 35,000 watch the Phillies beat the NY Giants 4-3
KNX-AM in Los Angeles CA begins radio transmissions
Bloody street battles between Nazis, socialist & police in Vienna
New York state revokes its Mullan-Gage Act Prohibition law; federal Volstead Act still in place
German Republic election: fascists & communists win
VIII Summer Olympic Games open at Olympic Stadium of Colombes, Paris, France
League of Nations conference on arms control and poison gas usage
First balloon flight over 40,000 feet takes place at Scott Field, Illinois
Nicaragua agrees to a US supervised presidential election in 1928
Mustafa Kemal Pasja becomes Turkish president
Pulitzer prize for the novel awarded to Harold L. Davis  for "Honey in the Horn"
Protestant Douglas Hyde is the only nominee for the position of the first President of Ireland
21 "non-neutral" Nazis and Communists are arrested in the Netherlands
Battle of Coral Sea begins (1st naval battle fought solely in air) between Japanese, US and Australian navies and air forces
Food is first rationed in the US
German occupiers imprison 450 prominent Dutch as hostages
Pulitzer prize for a Novel awarded to Ellen Glasgow  for "In this our Life"
German forces in Bavaria surrender unconditionally to American commander Jacob L. Devers
5 die in a 2 day riot at Alcatraz prison in San Francisco bay
Leroy Anderson's "Sleigh Ride" is first performed, by the Boston Pops Orchestra, with Arthur Fiedler conducting
The Hague Court of Justice convicts Nazi SS officer in the Netherlands Hans Rauter of Crimes against Humanity (executed 24 March 1949)
Plane carrying the entire Torino Serie A soccer squad crashes on the outskirts of Turin, Italy; 31 killed; Torino awarded League title at the request of their rivals
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Island
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll
Alan Freed hosts "Rock n' Roll Show" 1st prime-time network rock show, cancelled after 4 episodes
Alberto Lleras Camargo chosen as president of Colombia
"The 400 Blows", French film directed by François Truffaut, starring Jean-Pierre Léaud, is released
Pulitzer Prize for Drama awarded toArchibald Macleishfor his play "J.B."
First Great Delta Dam closes in North-South Beveland, Netherlands
CORE begins freedom rides from Washington, D.C.
First on-the-road Spacemobile lecture is given
Malcolm Ross and Victor Prather reach a record height of 34,668 meters in a balloon
South Africa ANC leader John Nkadimeng arrested
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
Pitcher Bob Shaw sets record of 5 balks in a game
"Another World" premieres on TV in the US
70 GATT-countries confer in Geneva
KIII TV channel 3 in Corpus Christi, TX (ABC) begins broadcasting
Pulitzer prize for General Non-Fiction awarded to Richard Hofstadter for "Anti-intellectualism"
Soviet government signs accord about building Fiat factory in USSR
Lunar Orbiter 4 launched by US; begins orbiting Moon May 7
Dancer Image disqualified due to drugs after winning 94th Kent Derby in 2:02½
Charles Gordone's "No Place to be Somebody" premieres in NYC
Stanley Cup Final, St. Louis Arena, St. Louis, MO: John Ferguson scores the winner as Montreal Canadiens edge St Louis Blues, 2-1 for a 4 game series sweep
National Guard kills 4 student anti-war protesters at Kent State University in Kent, Ohio
Pulitzer prize for General Non-Fiction awarded to Erik H. Erikson for "Gandhi's Truth"
Vietcong forms revolutionary government in Quang Tri, South Vietnam
BPAA US Women's Bowling Open won by Millie Martorella
Longest baseball game in Philadelphia's Veterans Stadium, Phillies beat Braves 5-4 in 20 innings
Patriarch Shenuda II of Kopitisch church visits the pope
PBS is the first TV network to show female nudity in "Steambath," featuring Valerie Perrine
Wings release "Red Rose Speedway" in UK
Ed Bullins's stage drama "The Taking of Miss Janie" opens in NYC
Houston's Bob Watson scores baseball's one-millionth run of all time
Italian film "Seven Beauties" directed by Lina Wertmüller, starring Giancarlo Giannini, and Shirley Stoler released - first film directed by a women to be nominated for an Academy Award
Schiedam train accident kills 24 people and injures 11
US Catholic bishops rescind automatic excommunications for divorced and remarried Catholics (receiving communion still outlawed if the previous marriages were not annulled by Church tribunals)
Russian leader Brezhnev visits West-Germany
NASA launches Fltsatcom-2, a communications system for US Navy aircraft, ships, submarines, and ground stations
Dodgers bat out of order against Phillies in 1st inning
White Sox 1st baseman Mike Squires catches final inning of 11-1 loss to Brewers, becoming 1st lefty to catch since Cubs Dale Long in 1958
Rockline premieres on KLOS FM in Los Angeles
Silvana Cruciata runs 15k female world record (49:44.0)
Yankee Ron Davis strikes out 8 consecutive Angels, ran record of 13 strikeouts of last 14 faced, also saved Gene Nelsons 1st win, 4-2
British destroyer HMS Sheffield hit by Exocet rocket off Falkland Islands: 20 of her crew died.
Twins rookie outfielder Jim Eisenreich, who suffers from Tourette's Syndrome, removes himself, due to taunts from Red Sox bleacher fans
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC

Itaipu Dam begins generating electricity on the border between Paraguay and Brazil,  - the world's second largest generator of electricity (considered one of the seven engineering wonders of the modern world)
President Babrak Karmal resigns as party leader of Afghanistan
Dufuna Canoe - the oldest known boat in Africa, 8,000-8,500 years old, is discovered in Dufuna, northeastern, Nigeria
PEPCON chemical plant in Henderson, Nevada explodes killing 2 and injuring 372 causing damage within 10-mile (16 km) radius
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Junior Felix of Toronto Blue Jays becomes 53rd MLB player to hit HR on 1st at bat
NASA launches Magellan probe to Venus from Space Shuttle Atlantis
NASA launches space shuttle Atlantis (STS-30) from the Kennedy Space Center, Florida
Latvia's parliament votes 138-0 (1 abstention) for Independence
MLB Baltimore Orioles Gregg Olson sets relief pitcher record of 41 consecutive scoreless innings
Pakistan beat Australia by 36 runs to win Austral-Asia Cup, Sharjah
ABC Masters Bowling Tournament won by Doug Kent
MLB Cleveland Indians' Chris James sets club record for most RBIs in a game (9)
MLB New York Mets Mackey Sasser & Mark Carreon are 8th teammates to hit consecutive pinch-hit HRs
US politician Mo Udall, Representative for Arizona (1961-91), resigns due to Parkinson disease
"I Saw the Light" single released by Wynonna (Billboard Song of the Year, 1992)
Greg Pavlik one-hits Tigers making the Rangers 1st AL team to pitch back-to-back one-hitters since the Washington Senators in 1917
The Jean-Marie Tjibaou Cultural Centre, designed by Renzo Piano to celebrate indigenous Kanak culture, is inaugurated near Nouméa, New Caledonia
Ken Livingstone becomes the first Mayor of London
The Milwaukee Art Museum addition, the first Santiago Calatrava-designed structure in the United States, opens to the public
Greensburg, Kansas is almost completely destroyed by a 1.7m wide EF-5 tornado
The Scottish National Party wins the Scottish general election and becomes the largest party in the Scottish Parliament for the first time ever
14 decapitated bodies and 9 hung from a bridge are found in Nuevo Laredo, Mexico
39 people are killed at a funeral in Wukari, Nigeria
5 US soldiers are killed by a bomb in Kandahar, Afghanistan
77 people are killed by the Syrian Army in Baniyas
Juventus F.C. win their 29th Serie A football title
Juan Carlos Varela is elected President of Panama
World Snooker Championship, Crucible Theatre, Sheffield: Stuart Bingham beats fellow Englishman Shaun Murphy, 18-15 for his lone world title
Ohio Governor John Kasich bows out of the race for US Republican Presidential nominee
California overtakes Great Britain to become the worlds fifth largest economy
Countries in Europe begin cautious reopening after COVID-19 lockdown, Italy restarts construction and takeaways, Germany reopens schools
India begins lifting some restrictions in regions with fewer COVID-19 cases
World leaders pledge $8 billion to research treatments and a vaccine for COVID-19, with the US and Russia not taking part
Malian woman Halima Cisse gives birth to nonuplets (nine babies) in Morocco, in only the third known case worldwide
First of its kind study of transgender children, by Princeton University, transitioning aged 3-12, found vast majority continue to identify with their new gender five years later
US Federal Reserve makes largest interest rate increase since 2000 (+0.5%), attempting to combat the fastest rate of inflation in four decades
"Primary" Trust" written by Eboni Booth and starring William Jackson Harper premieres at the Roundabout Theater in New York - 2024 Pulitzer Prize for Drama
In 2022 Covid-19 slipped from third to fourth leading cause of death in the US (behind heart disease, cancer and unintentional injuries) according to National Center for Health Statistics
WHO declares Covid-19 over as a global health emergency, but remains a significant threat, with seven million known deaths (real total likely to be 20 million)
Nearly three weeks of uninterrupted 24-hour live streaming (478 hours) of Sweden's 'Great Moose Migration' end after moose cross the Ångerman River
Five Carthusian monks from London Charterhouse monastery hung, drawn and quartered at Tyburn, London, for refusing to acknowledgeHenry VIIIas head of the Church of England
KingJames IIorders his Declaration of Indulgence read in English churches
George Frideric Handel's opera "Tolomeo, re di Egitto" premieres in London
American Academy of Arts & Science founded in Boston, James Bowdoin, John andSamuel Adamsfounding members
William Herschelreports seeing a red glow near lunar crater Aristarchus

Fourth Anglo-Mysore War: The Battle of Seringapatam ends the Siege of Seringapatam when the city is assaulted and the Sultan of MysoreTipu Sultanis killed by the besieging British army, under the command of General George Harris
KingFerdinand VIIof Spain signs the Decree of the 4th of May, returning Spain to absolutism
George McClellanhalts his troops before Yorktown, VA as the town is full of armed torpedoes left by CS Brigadier General Gabrial Rains
GeneralUlysses S. Grant's Union Army at Potomac attacksRobert E. Lee's Confederates at Rappahannock River
Wilfrid Laurierpasses the Naval Service Act, which creates the Royal Canadian Navy
Lou Gehrighits three consecutive HRs, Yankees 11, Tigers 9
Al Caponeenters Atlanta Penitentiary convicted of income tax evasion
Pulitzer Prize for poetry awarded toArchibald Macleishfor "Conquistador"
NLFord Frickdemonstrates revised balata ball to reporters by bouncing it on his office carpet ball proves to be 50% livelier
MGM releases film adaptation of "Gaslight", starring Charles Boyer,Ingrid Bergman, and an 18-year-oldAngela Lansburyin her film debut
German forces in Denmark, Norway and the Netherlands surrender unconditionally to British Field MarshalBernard Montgomeryat Luneburg Heath
Pulitzer Prize for Literature awarded toErnest Hemingwayfor "The Old Man and The Sea"
Queen Julianaunveils National Monument to Dams in Amsterdam
Anne FrankFoundation forms in Amsterdam
First Grammy Awards: Perry Como andElla Fitzgeraldwin major awards
San Francisco GiantsWillie Mayshit 512th career home run breaking Mel Ott's 29 year-old National League record, off of Los Angeles Dodgers Claude Osteen at Candlestick Park
Soviet PremierAlexei Kosyginconfirms the existence of Russian military advisors in Egypt
Margaret Thatcherbecomes the first woman to be elected Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Dave Kingman's fly ball never comes down (stuck in Metrodome ceiling)
Angela Bowie reveals that ex-husbandDavid Bowieslept withMick Jagger
Phil Collins, Al Jarreau, Ikutaro Kakehashi and Joe Zawinul receive honorary doctorate degrees from the Berklee College of Music in Boston
US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bushis hospitalized for erratic heartbeat
SingerCourtney Lovecleared of drug charges
A federal judge in Sacramento, California, gives "Unabomber"Ted Kaczynskifour life sentences plus 30 years after Kaczynski accepts a plea agreement sparing him from the death penalty.
"The Mummy" film directed by Stephen Sommers starringBrendan Fraser,Rachel Weiszand John Hannah premieres in the US
Barry Bondshits his 400th home run as an SF Giant, in a 3-0 win over Cincinnati. Bonds is first player with 400 homers for one team and 100 with another (Pirates)
Seth MacFarlanereaches an agreement worth $100 million with Fox to keep "Family Guy" and "American Dad" on television until 2012, making him the world's highest-paid television writer
Julia Louis-Dreyfusreceives the 2,407th star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, although on the original star her name is spelled incorrectly
Floyd Mayweather Jr.defeats Robert Guerrero to retain his WBC Welterweight title
Harper Leefiles a lawsuit against a literary agent over the copyright of "To Kill a Mockingbird"
Golden State WarriorStephen Curryis named MVP for the 2014-15 NBA season
LA Angels 1st basemanAlbert Pujolscollects his 3,000th MLB hit in a 5-0 win v Mariners in Seattle
Mexican boxer Canelo Alvarez unifies a trio of middleweight world titles in a close, unanimous decision over Daniel Jacobs at T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas, NV
Mexico's PresidentAndrés Manuel López Obradormakes an historic apology to the Mayan people for abuses against them in the five centuries since the Spanish conquest
US PresidentJoe Bidenannounces new goal of 160 million people, nearly of 70% of adults, vaccinated by July 4
Actress and writerCarrie Fisher, best known as Princess Leia from the Star Wars films, is honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame
Largest concert ofMadonna's career as she ends her "Celebration" world tour with a free concert in front of 1.6 million at Copacabana Beach, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
US PresidentDonald Trumpannounces 100% tariffs on foreign films brought into the United States

Battle of Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire: Final battle between the Houses of Lancaster and York sees Prince of Wales, Edward of Westminster, killed and KingEdward IVreturn to his throne, restoring political stability to England until his death in 1483
Thomas Edison's phonograph is shown for the first time at the Grand Opera House in NYC
Charles Rollsmeets Henry Royce at the Midland Hotel in Manchester, England. Go on to form the car manufacturer Roll-Royce.
